cards.name: [P1] Lightspeed Gaming - Moritz Baier-Lentz
cards.description:
**Fund:** Lightspeed Gaming & Interactive Media
**Partner:** Moritz Baier-Lentz
**Email:** moritz@lsvp.com
**Fit Score:** 92/100
**Stage:** Seed to Series A
**Check Size:** $500K - $5M+
**AUM:** $29B+ total Lightspeed, $6.5B+ gaming pool
**Tags:** gaming, AI-infrastructure, seed, series-a

**Fit Breakdown:**
- Gaming Native Fund: 20/20
- AI Infrastructure Focus: 20/20
- Deep Operational Expertise: 19/20
- Strategic Portfolio Alignment: 18/20
- Check Size Flexibility: 15/20

**Key Investments:**
- Epic Games (Unreal Engine, Fortnite, $31.5B valuation)
- Stability AI (generative AI, $1B valuation)
- Inworld AI (AI character engine, Series B in under 2 years)
- Theorycraft Games (AI-powered game studio)
- Lightforge Games (RPG with AI elements)

**Outreach Angle:**
Moritz is a literal gaming pro (Diablo II global #1) who traded virtual items for college tuition—he deeply understands AI's potential in gaming. Lightspeed's portfolio including Epic Games, Stability AI, and Inworld AI demonstrates their conviction in AI-gaming intersections. Their 'Gaming x AI Market Map' research shows active thought leadership.

---

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: moritz@lsvp.com
Subject: From Diablo II #1 to Inworld AI → the AI-native gaming thesis continues

Hi Moritz,

Your journey from Diablo II world #1 to building Lightspeed's Gaming & Interactive Media practice is exactly the kind of founder-backstory that signals genuine conviction. When someone trades virtual items for college tuition, they don't just understand gaming—they understand the economic and behavioral layers that make virtual worlds work.

Lightspeed's thesis on AI at the intersection of spatiotemporal reasoning and gaming infrastructure is playing out across your portfolio. Inworld AI's character engine, Stability AI's generative foundation, Theorycraft's AI-powered studio—you're systematically betting on intelligence as the next core game system. NEXUS AI fits squarely in that thesis.

We're building the runtime infrastructure that enables persistent AI agents in multiplayer worlds—think world models that evolve, action policies that learn from player behavior, and dynamic environments that maintain coherence at scale. The technology gaming has been promising for years but couldn't deliver because inference costs and state management made it economically impossible.

Your 'Gaming x AI Market Map' research shows you're thinking three moves ahead. I'd love to show you how NEXUS enables the next wave of portfolio companies to ship AI-native experiences without the infrastructure headaches.

Worth 15 minutes to explore the fit?

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
Lucas Fulks
Founder, NEXUS AI

---

**Status:** Ready to send
**Due Date:** 2026-03-01

cards.name: [P2] Play Ventures - Harri Manninen
cards.description:
**Fund:** Play Ventures
**Partner:** Harri Manninen
**Email:** harri@playventures.vc
**Fit Score:** 90/100
**Stage:** Pre-seed to Series A
**Check Size:** $250K - $2M
**Recent Fund:** Fund II - $140M (December 2025)
**Tags:** gaming, AI-infrastructure, pre-seed, seed

**Fit Breakdown:**
- Gaming Native Fund: 20/20
- AI Infrastructure Focus: 19/20
- Fresh Capital 2025: 18/20
- Founder Operational Experience: 18/20
- Check Size Alignment: 15/20

**Key Investments:**
- Scenario (AI game asset generation)
- Appcharge (mobile gaming payments, $58M Series B)
- Metica (AI gaming, $9M Series A)
- Huuuge Games (IPO, $1.2B valuation - angel)
- Mighty Bear Games (Singapore studio)

**Outreach Angle:**
Play Ventures' freshly raised $140M Fund II (Dec 2025) is actively deploying in AI-enhanced gaming. Harri co-founded Rocket Pack (acquired by Disney) and Matchmade, giving him deep operational empathy. Their 'B2B, AI & Infrastructure' focus directly aligns with NEXUS AI's platform approach.

---

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: harri@playventures.vc
Subject: Fund II deployment + Scenario → AI infrastructure for the next wave

Hi Harri,

Congratulations on the $140M Fund II close in December. Deploying fresh capital into AI-enhanced gaming tools while other funds are retreating to safer bets shows real conviction—and your portfolio proves it wasn't just talk.

Scenario's AI asset generation, Metica's AI gaming infrastructure, Appcharge's payments platform—you're building the tooling layer that enables the next generation of studios to compete. But there's a gap in that stack that keeps coming up in my conversations with gaming founders: the runtime layer for AI-powered worlds.

NEXUS AI is that infrastructure. We enable persistent AI agents, procedural narrative systems, and dynamic world evolution in multiplayer environments—without the compute costs that kill margins. Studios using our platform are cutting AI runtime expenses by 60% while shipping features they couldn't build before.

Your background founding Rocket Pack (acquired by Disney) and Matchmade gives you the operational empathy most VCs lack. You know the difference between demo AI and production AI that actually ships. I'd love 15 minutes to show you what we're building for the founders in your next cohort.

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
Lucas Fulks
Founder, NEXUS AI

---

**Status:** Ready to send
**Due Date:** 2026-03-01

cards.name: [P3] 359 Capital - Michael Spirito
cards.description:
**Fund:** 359 Capital (formerly Sapphire Sport)
**Partner:** Michael Spirito
**Email:** michael@359cap.com
**Fit Score:** 88/100
**Stage:** Seed to Series C
**Check Size:** $500K - $5M
**Recent Fund:** Fund II - $181M (November 2025 spinout)
**Tags:** gaming, AI-infrastructure, strategic-lp-network, seed

**Fit Breakdown:**
- Gaming AI Thesis: 19/20
- Strategic LP Network: 18/20
- Consumer Focus: 17/20
- Recent AI Investments: 18/20
- Check Size Alignment: 16/20

**Key Investments:**
- Jabali (AI game engine, $5M seed, 359 led)
- Backbone (gaming controller platform, unicorn)
- Manticore Games (Core platform, acquired)
- Betty (AI gaming, acquired)

**Outreach Angle:**
359 Capital's recent investment in Jabali (AI game engine) demonstrates explicit conviction in AI-gaming infrastructure. Their unique LP network of sports/media/entertainment brands provides distribution advantages for AI-powered interactive content. The spinout from Sapphire gives them fresh capital and autonomy.

---

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: michael@359cap.com
Subject: Jabali + strategic LP network → distribution for AI game infrastructure

Hi Michael,

The Jabali investment made headlines for good reason—an AI-native game engine backed by strategic LPs from City Football Group to adidas shows 359 Capital understands that gaming infrastructure needs more than just capital. It needs distribution.

Your LP base of iconic sports, media, and entertainment brands gives portfolio companies something most gaming VCs can't offer: immediate access to audiences, IP, and partnership channels. For AI infrastructure plays like NEXUS AI, that strategic advantage is invaluable.

We're building the runtime layer for AI-powered game worlds—enabling dynamic environments, persistent agents, and procedural content that evolves based on player behavior. Jabali empowers creators with no-code tools; NEXUS handles the hard infrastructure problem of running AI at scale in production multiplayer environments.

Given 359's focus on consumer-adjacent opportunities and your recent spinout from Sapphire, you're positioned to move quickly on gaming+AI infrastructure. I'd love to explore whether NEXUS fits your thesis and how your LP network could accelerate our go-to-market.

15 minutes next week?

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
Lucas Fulks
Founder, NEXUS AI

---

**Status:** Ready to send
**Due Date:** 2026-03-02

**Outreach Angle:**
TGF was named the most active early-stage gaming VC in 2024. Ilya and Maria's backgrounds at MGVC (MY.GAMES corporate venture arm) gave them deep gaming investment experience. Their investment in Layer AI demonstrates explicit AI-gaming thesis. The fund's outsized raise ($48M vs $30M target) shows LP conviction.

---

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: ilya@thegames.fund
Subject: Most active gaming VC 2024 → Layer AI and the infrastructure thesis

Hi Ilya,

Being named the most active early-stage gaming VC in 2024 isn't just a badge—it's proof that TGF's finger is on the pulse of where the industry is heading. Your investment in Layer AI ($6.5M seed) shows you see what I'm seeing: AI isn't just changing content creation, it's fundamentally changing what's possible in game worlds.

Layer enables smarter game art workflows. But studios still struggle with the runtime infrastructure to actually deploy AI at scale in production multiplayer environments. That's the gap NEXUS AI fills—we're the engine that enables persistent AI agents, evolving world states, and procedural systems without crushing unit economics.

Your background at MGVC (MY.GAMES corporate venture arm) and the fund's outsized raise ($48M vs $30M target) signal LP conviction in your gaming thesis. With Sergey Titov's technical expertise and your team's operational depth, you understand better than most that every content revolution needs a corresponding infrastructure layer.

We're already seeing 10x efficiency gains with early studio partners. Worth a 15-minute conversation to explore how NEXUS fits TGF's infrastructure thesis?

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
Lucas Fulks
Founder, NEXUS AI

---

**Due Date:** 2026-03-02

**Fit Breakdown:**

**Key Investments:**
- Sett (AI-powered storytelling, $15M Series A with Bessemer)
- InnPlay Labs (casual mobile gaming)
- Brain Games (cognitive gaming)
- Vgames Growth Financing portfolio

**Outreach Angle:**
Vgames' investment in Sett (AI-powered storytelling) demonstrates their conviction in AI-gaming intersections. Eitan's background leading Google Games gives him unique perspective on platform-scale gaming infrastructure. The non-dilutive growth financing option provides founders flexibility—relevant for AI infrastructure plays with different capital needs.

---

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: eitan@vgames.vc
Subject: Sett + non-dilutive growth financing → flexibility for AI infrastructure

Hi Eitan,

Your investment in Sett ($15M Series A alongside Bessemer) signals real conviction in AI-powered storytelling for mobile games. But what caught my attention about Vgames isn't just the thesis alignment—it's the unique capital structure you offer founders.

Non-dilutive growth financing alongside traditional equity is exactly what AI infrastructure companies need. We're building the runtime layer for persistent AI agents in multiplayer worlds—technology that requires significant compute and R&D investment before reaching scale. The flexibility to access growth capital without excessive dilution is a genuine differentiator for gaming founders.

Your background building the Google Games team in Tel Aviv gives you unique perspective on platform-scale gaming infrastructure. You understand that data and art are converging through AI—and that infrastructure is what bridges those disciplines at scale.

With the fresh $82M Fund II (Dec 2024), you're actively deploying. I'd love to show you how NEXUS AI enables the kind of AI-powered gaming experiences Vgames is betting on—and explore how your financing structure could work for our roadmap.

15 minutes for an intro call?

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
Lucas Fulks
Founder, NEXUS AI

---

**Due Date:** 2026-03-03
